query: Recent PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan ADIZ in May-June 2026, and reported pause or drop in activity. What are typical daily counts recently and highest single-day count in 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's my synthesis:

## PLA Aircraft Activity Around Taiwan's ADIZ (May–June 2026)

**Overall trajectory:** PLA aircraft activity in the first half of 2026 has been the lowest for each corresponding month since January 2023 — substantially below 2024–2025 levels. Critically, while *air* activity dropped, *naval* activity surged to record levels, indicating a shift in the mode of pressure rather than reduced overall presence.

### The pause/drop
- A **rare ~2-week pause** occurred **Feb 27–March 12, 2026**, with **zero** PLA aircraft crossing the median line — described by CNN and others as historically striking. Analysts (CSIS's Brian Hart, GMU's Taiwan Security Monitor) attributed it mainly to routine variability plus China's "Two Sessions" timing.
- Beyond that pause, air sorties stayed depressed through May–June.

### Typical recent daily counts
Late June/early July 2026 daily aircraft counts have been **single digits to low teens** on most days:
- Jun 25–26: 2 · Jun 28–29: **0** · Jul 1: 13 · Jul 2: 5 · Jul 4: 8 · Jul 6: 5 · Jul 7: 4

Occasional spikes reached ~20+ around patrol days (e.g., May 24–26 saw ~60 aircraft over three days, including a Y-20 tanker refueling J-16s).

### Highest single-day count in 2026
- **36 aircraft on March 17, 2026** — the highest of the year. **No day in April, May, or June 2026 exceeded 30 sorties.**

### Monthly aggregates (analyst K. Tristan Tang, from MND data)
- March: 171 sorties (lowest since Aug 2022) · June: 214 sorties, 134 median-line crossings
- **Naval contrast:** 225 PLAN vessels in June 2026 — highest June on record.

Full structured findings (key facts, sources with access dates, and quantitative data points) have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: All Quiet in the Taiwan Strait? Explaining the Recent Drop in PLA Aircraft Activity Around Taiwan
sources.snippet: From Feb 27 to March 5, MND reported no PLA aircraft near the ADIZ; unusually long pause. Prior activity included 19 aircraft Jan 29, 32 aircraft Feb 12.

sources.title: K. Tristan Tang: Monthly Update on PLA Air and Maritime Maneuvers Around Taiwan (June 2026)
sources.snippet: June 2026: 134 median line crossings, 214 total aircraft sorties, both substantially lower than 2024/2025. 225 PLAN vessels — highest June on record.

sources.title: K. Tristan Tang: Monthly Update on PLA Air and Maritime Maneuvers Around Taiwan (May 2026)
sources.snippet: Jan–May 2026 each lowest for corresponding month since Jan 2023. No day in May exceeded 30 sorties; last time above 30 was March 17.

sources.title: March 2026 PLA aircraft sorties around Taiwan
sources.snippet: March 2026: 171 total sorties, lowest since Aug 2022 post-Pelosi surge; peak of 36 aircraft on March 17, 2026.

sources.title: China's Y-20 Tanker Intercepted while Refueling J-16 Fighters Near Taiwan
sources.snippet: Between May 24 and 26, China flew nearly 60 military aircraft in Taiwan's ADIZ; 21 aircraft tracked May 25 including a Y-20 tanker refueling J-16s.

sources.title: For nearly two weeks, Chinese fighter jets stopped buzzing Taiwan. No one seems to know why.
sources.snippet: Aircraft suddenly stopped coming for nearly two weeks; broken March 12 with five PLA aircraft over previous 24 hours.

sources.title: Chinese warplanes return to skies near Taiwan after rare pause
sources.snippet: Since Feb 27, Chinese military flights near Taiwan's ADIZ unusually low; activity starting to pick back up.

sources.title: Taiwan detects rise in Chinese military incursions (July 1, 2026)
sources.snippet: July 1: 13 sorties of PLA aircraft, 10 PLAN vessels; 9 crossed median line.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 4 Chinese aircraft, 9 naval vessels (July 7, 2026)
sources.snippet: July 7: 4 sorties of PLA aircraft, 9 PLAN vessels, 3 crossed median line.

sources.title: Taiwan detects spiked Chinese incursions (July 4, 2026)
sources.snippet: July 4: 8 sorties of PLA aircraft, 10 PLAN vessels, 7 official ships.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (June 26, 2026) — ROC MND
sources.snippet: Jun 25–26: 2 sorties of PLA aircraft and 6 PLAN ships detected.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (June 29, 2026) — ROC MND
sources.snippet: Jun 28–29: 7 PLAN ships and 3 official ships; no PLA aircraft detected.

sources.title: The Mysterious Dip in China's Military Flights Around Taiwan
sources.snippet: Unusually low Chinese flights coincided with the 'two sessions'; CSIS's Brian Hart dismissed the hype.

data_points.metric: Highest single-day PLA aircraft count in 2026 (March 17)
June 2026 PLAN vessels detected (highest June on record)
Duration of rare pause with zero PLA aircraft (Feb 27–March 12)
PLAN + Coast Guard vessels around First Island Chain (May 23)
